description: Comprehensive UX & design overhaul to transform Slak.me into a trustworthy, navigable civic information tool with improved navigation, visual hierarchy, search, and content discovery

userStories.title: Header tagline and brand identity
userStories.description: As a first-time visitor, I want to immediately understand what Slak.me does so that I can trust the site as a credible information source.
acceptanceCriteria: Tagline 'Independent Executive Order Analysis' appears below the 'Slak.me' brand name in src/app/_components/theme/header.tsx
Tagline uses text-xs text-muted-foreground styling, visible but not competing with brand
Header has a bottom border (border-b border-border) for visual structure
Header bottom margin reduced from mb-12 to mb-8
Site metadata description in src/app/layout.tsx updated to: 'Independent analysis of executive orders, legislation, and government actions — tracking constitutional risk and civic impact'
Tagline visible on both desktop and mobile layouts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Files to modify: src/app/_components/theme/header.tsx, src/app/layout.tsx

userStories.title: Install breadcrumb component and add to document pages
userStories.description: As a user who landed directly on a document page, I want to see breadcrumb navigation so that I understand where I am in the site and can navigate to related areas.
acceptanceCriteria: Install shadcn breadcrumb component via: pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add breadcrumb
Create src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/breadcrumbs.tsx component
Breadcrumbs render above the document title in src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/page.tsx: Home > {Document Type} > {Truncated Title}
Home links to /
Document type segment is human-readable (e.g., 'Executive Orders' not 'EXECUTIVE_ORDER')
Document title truncated to ~60 characters with ellipsis
Breadcrumbs visible on both desktop and mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Files to create: src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/breadcrumbs.tsx. Files to modify: src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/page.tsx

userStories.title: Artifact styles config map in document-utils
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a centralized config mapping artifact titles to border colors and icon components so that visual differentiation can be applied consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Add artifactStyles config map to src/lib/document-utils.ts mapping artifact titles to border class strings and Lucide icon component names

Mappings: ELI5 -> border-blue-400 + BookOpen, Key Points -> border-slate-400 + ListChecks, Areas of Concern -> border-orange-400 + AlertTriangle, Constitutional Considerations -> border-red-400 + Scale, Take Action -> border-green-500 + Megaphone, Social Post -> border-indigo-400 + Share2, Letter of Concern -> border-amber-400 + Mail, Risk Score Details -> border-red-500 + ShieldAlert, Final Summary -> border-primary + FileText
Export a helper function getArtifactStyle(title: string) that returns the style config or a default
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File to modify: src/lib/document-utils.ts. Data-only, no UI changes. Next story applies it to the component.

userStories.title: Apply artifact visual differentiation to sections
userStories.description: As a user scanning a document page, I want each analysis section to have a distinct visual identity so that I can quickly find the type of content I'm looking for.
acceptanceCriteria: Each artifact section in src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/artifact-section.tsx displays a colored left border (border-l-4) using the artifactStyles config from US-003
Each artifact section title shows a Lucide icon (h-4 w-4) next to the title text in the CollapsibleTrigger
Border color applied to the content container div (the bg-muted box)
Colors work correctly in both light and dark themes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003. File to modify: src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/artifact-section.tsx. Import getArtifactStyle from document-utils.

userStories.title: Section preview text when collapsed
userStories.description: As a user scanning a document page, I want to see a brief preview of each section's content when collapsed so that I can decide which sections to read without opening each one.
acceptanceCriteria: When an artifact section is collapsed, the first ~120 characters of content appear below the title in src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/artifact-section.tsx
Preview text is plain text with markdown formatting stripped (use simple regex to remove markdown syntax)
Preview styled as text-sm text-muted-foreground truncate
Preview disappears when section is expanded
Preview does not add significant height to collapsed sections (single line, truncated)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File to modify: src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/artifact-section.tsx

userStories.title: Artifact section list with expand/collapse all
userStories.description: As a user reading a document analysis, I want to expand or collapse all sections at once so that I can either read everything sequentially or scan just the headers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new client component src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/artifact-section-list.tsx that wraps all artifact sections and manages collective open/close state
Expand All and Collapse All buttons appear above the artifact sections using existing Button component with variant='outline' and size='sm'
Clicking Expand All opens all sections; clicking Collapse All closes all
Individual sections can still be toggled independently after a bulk action
URL param ?sections=all supported for expand all via link sharing
Existing openSections URL param pattern preserved for individual sections
Update src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/page.tsx to use the new ArtifactSectionList wrapper instead of directly mapping artifacts
ArtifactSection component updated to accept isOpen and onToggle props from parent instead of managing own state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Files to create: src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/artifact-section-list.tsx. Files to modify: src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/page.tsx, src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/artifact-section.tsx. This lifts open/close state from ArtifactSection to the parent wrapper.

userStories.title: Migrate hardcoded grays to theme tokens
userStories.description: As a developer maintaining the codebase, I want all color values to use theme tokens so that the warm design tone applies consistently and theme switching works properly.
acceptanceCriteria: All instances of bg-gray-200 replaced with bg-muted across all component files
All instances of dark:bg-gray-800 removed (handled by bg-muted dark mode)
All instances of text-gray-500 and text-gray-600 replaced with text-muted-foreground
All instances of dark:text-gray-400 removed (handled by text-muted-foreground)
All instances of border-gray-700 and dark:border-gray-700 replaced with border-border
No hardcoded gray Tailwind classes remain in any component file (except where intentionally distinct from theme, e.g. specific badge colors)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — check both light and dark themes
userStories.notes: Search all files in src/app/_components/ and src/app/eo-summary/ for bg-gray, text-gray, border-gray, dark:bg-gray, dark:text-gray, dark:border-gray patterns.

userStories.title: Accessibility fixes for interactive elements
userStories.description: As a user relying on assistive technology, I want all interactive elements to be semantic and accessible so that I can navigate and use the site effectively.
acceptanceCriteria: All div elements with onClick handlers in ArtifactActions (src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/artifact-section.tsx) converted to semantic button elements
Each action button has an aria-label attribute: 'Copy link to section', 'Copy content', 'Share', 'Send via ResistBot'
animate-pulse on severe risk scores (8-10) in src/app/_components/risk-score.tsx replaced with static visual indicator: ring-2 ring-offset-2 using the risk color
All action buttons have visible focus styles via focus-visible ring
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Files to modify: src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/artifact-section.tsx (ArtifactActions component), src/app/_components/risk-score.tsx

userStories.title: Homepage full-text search component
userStories.description: As a user looking for a specific document, I want to search across all documents from the homepage so that I can quickly find relevant analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new client component src/app/_components/document-search.tsx with a prominent search input with Search icon (Lucide)
Input calls existing api.document.search tRPC procedure with 300ms debounce
Results appear in a dropdown/popover below the search input
Each result shows: title, risk score badge (reuse RiskScore component), type badge (reuse DocumentTypeBadge), and truncated short summary
Clicking a result navigates to /eo-summary/{slug}?sections=ELI5
Results capped at 30 items maximum
Empty query shows no results
No results state shows 'No documents found' message
Search input accessible with keyboard (arrow keys to navigate results, Enter to select)
Add the search component to the homepage in src/app/page.tsx, placed above the data table
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Files to create: src/app/_components/document-search.tsx. Files to modify: src/app/page.tsx. Reuses existing document.search tRPC procedure from src/server/api/routers/document.ts which is currently unused in frontend.

userStories.title: Sticky sidebar table of contents (desktop)
userStories.description: As a desktop user reading a long document analysis, I want a persistent table of contents sidebar so that I can see all available sections and jump to any one without scrolling.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new component src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/table-of-contents.tsx that renders a vertical list of section links
TOC items derived from artifactOrder (from src/lib/document-utils.ts) filtered to only artifacts present on current document
Document page artifact area in src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/page.tsx uses two-column grid layout: md:grid md:grid-cols-[200px_1fr] md:gap-6
TOC column is sticky: sticky top-4 so it stays visible during scroll
TOC is collapsible on desktop: a toggle button collapses to a narrow column showing longer dashes for section headings and shorter dashes for any subsections
Active section highlighted with a left-border accent using IntersectionObserver
Clicking a TOC item scrolls to the section AND opens it if collapsed (requires integration with ArtifactSectionList from US-006)
Risk score badge and document type badge displayed at top of TOC for quick reference
TOC hidden on mobile (only visible at md breakpoint and above)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-006 (ArtifactSectionList) for controlling section open/close. Files to create: src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/table-of-contents.tsx. Files to modify: src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/page.tsx

userStories.title: Mobile floating TOC with bottom sheet
userStories.description: As a mobile user reading a long document, I want a floating button that opens a section navigator so that I can jump between sections without scrolling back to the top.
acceptanceCriteria: Install shadcn sheet component via: pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add sheet
Create new component src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/mobile-toc-sheet.tsx
FAB positioned at fixed bottom-4 right-4 z-50 with List icon (Lucide), only visible below md breakpoint (md:hidden)
Tapping FAB opens a bottom sheet listing all artifact sections present on the document
Each sheet item shows the artifact icon and title (using artifactStyles from US-003)
Tapping a sheet item scrolls to the section, opens it if collapsed, and closes the sheet
Document page has pb-16 padding on mobile to prevent content behind the FAB
Sheet is accessible: can be closed with swipe down or close button
Add the mobile TOC component to src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/page.tsx
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 (artifactStyles) and US-006 (ArtifactSectionList). Files to create: src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/mobile-toc-sheet.tsx. Files to modify: src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/page.tsx

userStories.title: Homepage hero section and stat cards
userStories.description: As a first-time visitor to the homepage, I want to see a professional, informative hero section so that I immediately understand the site's purpose and can start exploring.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new component src/app/_components/hero-section.tsx that replaces the current personal intro paragraph in src/app/page.tsx
Hero contains: large serif heading (e.g., 'Track the Impact of Executive Orders'), concise sans-serif subheading (1-2 sentences, third person, not first person), and the search component from US-009
Create new component src/app/_components/stat-card.tsx built on existing Card component: icon + large number + label
Stats bar with 3 stat cards showing: total documents tracked, high-risk count (risk >= 5), date of most recent update — all server-rendered via props
Personal 'I built this' text removed from homepage (it already exists on the About page)
Hero looks professional in both light and dark themes
Responsive: stats stack vertically on mobile, horizontal on desktop
Update src/app/page.tsx to use the new hero-section component
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-009 (search component). Files to create: src/app/_components/hero-section.tsx, src/app/_components/stat-card.tsx. Files to modify: src/app/page.tsx

userStories.title: Homepage filter bar for data table
userStories.description: As a user browsing documents, I want to filter the data table by document type and risk level so that I can focus on the specific content I care about.
acceptanceCriteria: Document type filter added to src/app/_components/document-table/data-table.tsx using existing Select component with options: All Types, Executive Order, Fact Sheet, Remarks, Legislation, Other
Risk level filter using existing Select component with options: All Risk Levels, Low (0-2), Moderate (3-4), Elevated (5-6), High (7-8), Severe (9-10)
Filters placed inline with existing search input in flex flex-wrap gap-2 layout
Filter state synced to URL params (?type=EXECUTIVE_ORDER&risk=high) following existing pattern for sort/search/page in the same file
Section heading 'All Documents' added above the table
Results count shown: 'Showing X of Y documents'
Changing a filter resets pagination to page 1
Filters work in combination with existing search and sort
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File to modify: src/app/_components/document-table/data-table.tsx. Follow existing URL state sync pattern already in this file.

userStories.title: Redesign high-risk alert section and document cards
userStories.description: As a user viewing the homepage, I want the high-risk documents section to feel like a dashboard alert and the cards to convey more useful information at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Yellow warning box in src/app/page.tsx replaced with structured alert panel using AlertTriangle icon (Lucide) + 'High Risk Documents' header
High-risk count displayed as a large number with label, not buried in a sentence

Document cards in src/app/_components/document-card.tsx enhanced: DocumentTypeBadge added, date signed shown as small detail, left-border color accent based on risk level (using risk score color logic), 'Read More' ghost button removed (entire card is already a clickable link)
Alert panel and cards look professional in both light and dark themes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Files to modify: src/app/page.tsx, src/app/_components/document-card.tsx

userStories.title: Related documents tRPC procedure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a backend procedure to fetch related documents so the frontend can display them on document pages.
acceptanceCriteria: New tRPC procedure document.getRelated added to src/server/api/routers/document.ts
Input schema: { documentId: z.number(), type: z.nativeEnum(DocumentType), riskScore: z.number() }
Returns up to 4 published documents of same type OR similar risk score (+/-1), excluding the current document, ordered by dateSigned desc
Falls back to highest-risk published documents if no same-type matches found
Response includes a reason field indicating match type: 'type' or 'risk'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File to modify: src/server/api/routers/document.ts. Backend only, no UI changes. The reason field enables the frontend to show 'More Executive Orders' vs 'Similar Risk Level'.

userStories.title: Related documents UI section on document pages
userStories.description: As a user reading a document analysis, I want to see related documents so that I can explore connected executive orders and legislation without returning to the homepage.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new component src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/related-documents.tsx
Component calls document.getRelated tRPC procedure from US-015
Uses grid of existing DocumentCard components (reuse, don't duplicate)
Grid responsive: 1 column on mobile, 2 on tablet, 4 on desktop
Section title indicates the relation reason derived from the procedure's reason field (e.g., 'More Executive Orders' when matched by type, 'Similar Risk Level' when matched by risk)
Section placed in src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/page.tsx after artifact sections, before prev/next navigation
Section hidden if no related documents exist
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-015. Files to create: src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/related-documents.tsx. Files to modify: src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/page.tsx

userStories.title: Site context banner for direct-landing users

Banner includes two link buttons: 'Browse All Documents' (links to /) and 'About' (links to /about)
Dismiss button (X icon) hides the banner and saves dismissal to localStorage key 'slakme-banner-dismissed'
Banner does not reappear after dismissal (persists across page navigations)

Styled with bg-muted background, border, and rounded-md for consistency

userStories.title: Risk score size variants and tooltip
userStories.description: As a user viewing document details, I want a more informative risk score visualization so that I can immediately understand the severity and meaning of the score.
acceptanceCriteria: Install shadcn tooltip component via: pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add tooltip
Refactor src/app/_components/risk-score.tsx to support size prop: 'compact' (default) and 'full'
Compact variant: circle increased to h-8 w-8 with text-sm font-bold. Scores >= 7 get subtle ring-2 glow in risk color
Full variant: displays score at text-3xl font-bold, risk level label below (e.g., 'High'), horizontal meter bar (colored div with width proportional to score on 0-10 scale)
Tooltip on hover (both variants) explains the score range meaning (e.g., 'High Risk (7/10): This document poses significant constitutional concerns')
Details pane in src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/details-pane.tsx updated to use full variant
Table and cards continue using compact variant (no changes needed, compact is default)
Both variants work in light and dark themes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Files to modify: src/app/_components/risk-score.tsx, src/app/eo-summary/[slug]/_components/details-pane.tsx

userStories.description: As a user finishing a document analysis, I want the prev/next navigation to be more informative and visually prominent so that I'm encouraged to continue exploring.

Each link shows: document title, risk score badge (compact), and document type badge
Clickable area covers the entire card container
Previous and Next labels clearly visible above the document title
Responsive: cards stack vertically on mobile, side-by-side on desktop

Jump to section dropdown lists all artifact sections with their icons from artifactStyles

Sticky bar does not conflict with the main site header
Hidden on mobile where the floating TOC FAB serves a similar purpose (md:hidden on mobile, visible md and above)
